_comment: Provider/model registry powering per-interaction model selection. Every LLM call (requirement scoring, vote synthesis, chat) can pick any enabled model here. API keys are read from environment variables named in 'api_key_env' - they are NEVER stored in this file. Add or remove models freely; the backend exposes this list to the UI via GET /api/models.

models.label: Claude Opus 4.8
models.best_for: Deepest reasoning: final vote synthesis, contested Must gating, nuanced OpCo-fit judgment.

models.label: Claude Sonnet 4.6
models.best_for: High-volume per-requirement scoring at strong quality/cost balance (recommended default for scoring runs).

models.label: Claude Haiku 4.5
models.best_for: Fast, cheap chat answers and bulk first-pass classification.

models.label: GPT-5.5
models.best_for: OpenAI flagship reasoning model. Live scoring and the OpenAI side of the dual-provider vote.

models.label: GPT-4o
models.best_for: Alternative frontier reasoning; cross-check a second opinion against the primary model.

providers.name: Azure OpenAI
providers.note: For SSA/Bain enterprise tenants that route through Azure. Set AZURE_OPENAI_ENDPOINT and a deployment name as the model id.

providers.name: Offline Demo (no API key)
providers.note: Deterministic, rules-based stand-in so the whole app runs with ZERO API keys. Produces structured, persona-flavored output grounded in the bundled knowledge base. Clearly labeled 'demo' in every result.

models.label: Offline Demo Engine
models.best_for: Running the full pipeline and UI with no keys / no network. Not a substitute for a real model on live proposals.
